name: Regina Barzilay
sub_directions: Deep Learning for Natural Language Processing
Generative AI for Molecular and Protein Design
AI for Disease Prediction and Early Detection (e.g., cancer, flu)
Computational Biology and AI-driven Drug Discovery
Interpretability and Trustworthy AI in Medical Applications
tech_stack: Machine Learning models for predictive analytics
recent_papers: MIT scientists debut a generative AI model that could create molecules addressing hard-to-treat diseases
AI maps how a new antibiotic targets gut bacteria
MIT researchers develop AI tool to improve flu vaccine strain selection
interest_paragraph: I am particularly drawn to Professor Barzilay's pioneering work at the intersection of AI, computational biology, and medical applications. My interest is specifically piqued by the development of generative AI models for molecular design and drug discovery, as well as the application of AI for disease prediction and early detection, such as in lung cancer and flu vaccine selection. The innovative use of deep learning to tackle complex health challenges, as exemplified in papers like 'MIT scientists debut a generative AI model that could create molecules addressing hard-to-treat diseases,' aligns perfectly with my research aspirations.
